metadata.methodology: Self-employment counts are from the ONS Labour Force Survey and include anyone who classifies their main job as self-employed. Median weekly income is self-reported through the LFS. HMRC Self Assessment data provides income declarations but only for those who file returns. IPSE surveys supplement official data with sentiment measures.
knownIssues: Self-reported income in the LFS is known to understate actual self-employment earnings
The LFS experienced a significant response rate decline from 2020, reducing data quality
IR35 reforms in April 2021 shifted many self-employed workers to PAYE, affecting comparability
Gig economy workers may not self-classify as self-employed in surveys
COVID-19 caused a structural break in the series — 700,000 self-employed left the sector in 2020-21
